edges.description: R. conorii disseminates to and injures vascular endothelium.
Endothelial injury drives vasculitis and vascular leak.
Endothelial infection contributes to the characteristic exanthem.
Endothelial infection contributes to the maculopapular rash pattern.
Rickettsial endothelial injury directly manifests as vasculitis.
Vascular injury can produce petechial rash.
Microvascular injury contributes to acute kidney involvement.
Severe endothelial disease can involve the lungs.
Endothelial inflammatory cytokines contribute to systemic fever.
Vasoactive prostaglandins modulate the vascular inflammatory phenotype.

edges.source: Macrolide alternatives for children and pregnancy

nodes.description: Doxycycline is the treatment of choice for Mediterranean spotted fever and tick-borne rickettsioses.

evidence.reference_title: Mediterranean Spotted Fever: Current Knowledge and Recent Advances.
evidence.snippet: Doxycycline remains the treatment of choice

evidence.reference_title: Tick-borne rickettsioses in international travellers.
evidence.snippet: Doxycycline is the recommended treatment for tick-borne rickettsioses

nodes.description: Spotted-fever-group rickettsial infection induces endothelial COX-2 and vasoactive prostaglandin release, which may regulate inflammatory responses and vascular permeability changes.

evidence.reference_title: Infection of human endothelial cells with spotted Fever group rickettsiae stimulates cyclooxygenase 2 expression and release of vasoactive prostaglandins.
evidence.snippet: Rocky Mountain spotted fever and boutonneuse fever, due to Rickettsia rickettsii and R. conorii, respectively, are characterized by widespread infection of the vascular endothelium, microvascular injury, and vasculitis.

evidence.reference_title: Infection of human endothelial cells with spotted Fever group rickettsiae stimulates cyclooxygenase 2 expression and release of vasoactive prostaglandins.
evidence.snippet: Induction of the endothelial COX-2 system and the resultant enhanced release of vasoactive PGs may contribute to the regulation of inflammatory responses and vascular permeability changes during spotted fever rickettsioses.

nodes.description: R. conorii-infected human endothelial cells secrete IL-6 and IL-8 through a cell-associated IL-1 alpha-dependent pathway, providing an inflammatory mechanism for vasculitis and systemic febrile symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: IL-6 and IL-8 production from cultured human endothelial cells stimulated by infection with Rickettsia conorii via a cell-associated IL-1 alpha-dependent pathway.
evidence.snippet: human umbilical vein endothelial cells (HUVEC) infected with R. conorii actively secrete high levels of IL-8 and IL-6

evidence.reference_title: IL-6 and IL-8 production from cultured human endothelial cells stimulated by infection with Rickettsia conorii via a cell-associated IL-1 alpha-dependent pathway.
evidence.snippet: providing a possible mechanism for the vasculitis observed in Mediterranean spotted fever.

nodes.description: R. conorii targets vascular endothelial cells in vivo; endothelial damage is supported by circulating endothelial cells and elevated thrombomodulin and von Willebrand factor in patients.

evidence.reference_title: Demonstration of Rickettsia conorii-induced endothelial injury in vivo by measuring circulating endothelial cells, thrombomodulin, and von Willebrand factor in patients with Mediterranean spotted fever.
evidence.snippet: The endothelial cell (EC) is the primary target for Rickettsia conorii (RC) in Mediterranean spotted fever (MSF).

evidence.reference_title: Mediterranean Spotted Fever: Current Knowledge and Recent Advances.
evidence.snippet: All of these manifestations arise from the disseminated infection of the endothelium by R. conorii conorii.

nodes.description: Fever is a core manifestation after the incubation period.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0001945 | Fever | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Mediterranean spotted fever: case series of 24 years (1989-2012).
evidence.snippet: Most common symptoms were: fever (98%), myo-arthralgia (64%) and headache (48%).

nodes.description: Oral clarithromycin or azithromycin may be acceptable alternative treatment options, especially for children and during pregnancy.
nodes.id: Macrolide alternatives for children and pregnancy

evidence.reference_title: Epidemiology and clinical features of Mediterranean spotted fever in Italy.
evidence.snippet: Recent studies indicate that oral clarithromycin and azithromycin could constitute an acceptable alternative for the treatment of the disease in children; furthermore, they could be recommended during pregnancy.

nodes.description: The typical rash is maculopapular and spreads centripetally.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0040186 | Maculopapular exanthema | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Mediterranean spotted fever: case series of 24 years (1989-2012).
evidence.snippet: Maculopapular rash was noticed in 87%, affecting palms in 77% and soles in 69%.

nodes.description: The maculopapular rash may evolve into petechial lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0000967 | Petechiae | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: maculopapular rash, which may evolve into a petechial form

nodes.description: Acute renal injury or insufficiency is the most frequent complication in one large series.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0000083 | Renal insufficiency | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Mediterranean spotted fever: case series of 24 years (1989-2012).
evidence.snippet: Most frequent complication was acute renal injury.

nodes.description: Severe disease can rarely include respiratory failure.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0002878 | Respiratory failure | Very rare (<4-1%)

nodes.description: Infected endothelial cells and systemic endothelial injury produce generalized vasculitis, microvascular injury, and vascular permeability changes that underlie severe organ manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Tick-borne rickettsioses in international travellers.
evidence.snippet: potentially life-threatening disease with disseminated vaculitis is occasionally seen.

evidence.reference_title: Demonstration of Rickettsia conorii-induced endothelial injury in vivo by measuring circulating endothelial cells, thrombomodulin, and von Willebrand factor in patients with Mediterranean spotted fever.
evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations such as thrombosis and vasculitis are mediated by pathologic changes localized in blood vessels.

nodes.description: A generalized rash is a core clinical feature.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0000988 | Skin rash | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Tick-borne rickettsioses in international travellers.
evidence.snippet: flu-like illness typically accompanied by a cutaneous rash and an inoculation eschar at the site of the tick bite

nodes.description: Brown dog tick transmission introduces R. conorii subsp. conorii, an intracellular rickettsial bacterium, initiating acute spotted fever rickettsiosis.

evidence.reference_title: Mediterranean Spotted Fever: Current Knowledge and Recent Advances.
evidence.snippet: Mediterranean spotted fever (MSF) is an emerging tick-borne rickettsiosis of the spotted fever group (SFG)

nodes.description: Vasculitis reflects endothelial tropism and injury in severe disease.

evidence.reference_title: Boutonneuse fever
evidence.snippet: HP:0002633 | Vasculitis | Very rare (<4-1%)

evidence.reference_title: Tick-borne rickettsioses in international travellers.
evidence.snippet: potentially life-threatening disease with disseminated vaculitis is occasionally seen.
